tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
On the AWS sign-in page,
a. Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
b. Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Navigate to the Services menu at the top and click on S3 in the Storage section.2. In the left menu, choose Buckets, click Create Bucket and fill in the bucket details.
a. Bucket Type Select : General purposeb. Bucket Name: Enter whiz-batch-operation
Object ownership: Select ACLs Enabled option then Choose Object writer
Bucket settings for Block Public Access: Uncheck the option, Block all public access and select the check box option of Acknowledgment.
Leave other settings as default.
Click on the Create Bucket button.
Task 2 : Create S3 Bucket
Click on your bucket name.2. Click to download the ZIP file, then extract it and upload the files to the S3 bucket.
The uploaded files are now visible in the S3 bucket.
Click the photo1.jpg object to copy its Object URL, then paste the URL into a new browser tab.
Now we can’t see this because the object is not public.
Open Cloud Shell present in the top right corner.
Replace the bucket name
Click Ctrl+X, Y and then Click Enter
Now you can see: 'Manifest created. Uploaded manifest to S3.'
Go to the S3 bucket manifests/ to see the manifest file created: jpg_manifest.csv.
Under Scope, choose Use an existing manifest file, choose CSV, and in the Manifest object, select the S3 bucket where the manifest file is located. Then, click Next.
In the 'Choose Operation' field, select 'Replace access control list (ACL)'.
Task 4 : Create Batch Operation
Under 'Everyone (public access)', check the 'Read' checkbox.
Also, check the box acknowledging.
Task 4 : Create Batch Operation
Click Next
In the Configure Additional Options section, set the priority to 10.
Check the box for Generate completion report and select All tasks.
In the Source area, choose your bucket and add /batch-reports/jpg-acl-job/.
The Permissions field, choose batch_operation_role-<RANDOM-NUMBERS>, then click Next
Click Create Job.
Task 4 : Create Batch Operation
Wait for 2 minutes, then the created job will show 'Awaiting your confirmation to run'. Click to run the job.
Now you can see that the job has been successfully completed.
Now go to the bucket, copy the photo1.jpg object URL, and paste it into a new browser tab.
Now You can see this Image!
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the Right side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and shows you whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
sample output .
Task 4 : Create Batch Operation
You have successfully created a manifest file listing the target JPG files in S3.
You have configured an S3 Batch Operations job to update the ACL of those objects.
You have run the Batch Operations job, making the JPG files publicly accessible.
You have verified that the completion report confirms all objects were processed successfully.
Sign out of AWS Account.
You have successfully completed the lab.
Once you completed the steps, click on End Lab from your lab lab console and wait till the process gets completed.
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the Right side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and shows you whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
sample output .
Task 6 : Validation of the Lab
